word: G4258 - προαμαρτάνω - proamartánō - pro-am-ar-tan'-o - Sin already

sections.paragraph:
- Original: προαμαρτάνω

- Transliteration: Proamartano

- Phonetic: pro-am-ar-tan'-o

- Definition:

1. to sin before

a. of those who before receiving baptism had been guilty of the vices especially common among the Gentiles

- Origin: from G4253 and G264

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4253 and G264; to sin previously (to conversion): - sin already heretofore sin.
